context: PRD-001 review-path carve-out (ADR-023): the review system renders via the legacy makeSlides/createPageContent/jQuery/#content path, blocking its deletion in epic 001
choice: Created epic 003 to convert the review path to the PRD-001 Vue components and then remove the legacy renderer, npm jquery, gsap, mobile-drag-drop, and #content
rationale: Keeps epic 001 focused on the main quiz path and low-risk; review conversion reuses the new components so it is mostly wiring
alternatives: Expand epic 001 to convert review now

context: Both IndexPage (main path) and ReviewSession (review path) need the slide-type -> Vue component map
choice: Extracted a shared EXERCISE_COMPONENTS map into src/vue/components/exercise/exerciseComponents.ts; both pages import it
rationale: DRY; the TSK calls for review to use the same map as IndexPage
alternatives: Pass the map down as a prop

context: Review must collect per-slide results without persisting to course progress
choice: ReviewSession holds a local currentIndex and pushes {slideTxt, correct: evaluation.correct, total: evaluation.responses} via slide.evaluate() on Continue; never calls slide.saveData()
rationale: Exercise components already call evaluateAnswer() (setting slide.res) internally, so slide.evaluate() reflects the response; saving is the host's responsibility and review deliberately omits it
alternatives: Keep ReviewSessionController and its conclude monkey-patch
Collect results on the answer event instead of continue

context: The Slide base constructor wired createHtml + makeSlidesStrategy + evaluateStrategy + resultType; only evaluate/result are still used
choice: Removed createHtml and makeSlidesStrategy from the Slide constructor and SlideInterface; kept evaluateStrategy and resultType (used by slide.evaluate()/result() on both paths)
rationale: evaluate()/result() are framework-agnostic and still needed; createHtml/makeSlides only fed the deleted DOM renderer

context: buttons.ts (continueButton/doneButton/makeButton) and explainIcon.ts were legacy-renderer DOM helpers with zero live consumers after review conversion; continueButton targeted the now-deleted #slide
choice: Deleted buttons.ts, explainIcon.ts, and buttons.test.ts; removed their barrel re-exports
rationale: Part of removing the legacy renderer entirely (PRD-003 scope); leaving code that injects into a deleted element is dead and misleading
alternatives: Leave them as dead but compiling code

context: saveData.ts used jQuery $.extend(new Array(), raw) to clone the persisted saves array
choice: Replaced with [...(raw as SaveData[])] per TSK guidance (not Object.assign)
rationale: Spread yields a proper array copy with correct length; differential replay confirms storage state is byte-identical (modulo the wall-clock ts)
